mission.title: grpc: Graceful shutdown is not working for async server
mission.description: Graceful shutdown is not working for async server. This issue affects 14+ users.

steps.title: Identify grpc troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your grpc setup:
gRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)
Check your build output and test logs for errors related to this issue.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Graceful shutdown is not working for async server
steps.description:
`KeyboardInterrupt` exception will not be propagated into individual coroutines, instead it will be raised in the `asyncio.run` or `loop.run_until_complete` lines. This means my previous graceful shutdown example won't work, as reported in https://github.com/grpc/grpc/issues/26123.

There are 2
```yaml
import logging
import asyncio
import grpc


async def serve() -> None:
    server = grpc.aio.server()
    listen_addr = "[::]:50051"
    server.add_insecure_port(listen_addr)
    logging.info("Starting server on %s", listen_addr)
    await server.start()
    try:
        await server.wait_for_termination()
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        # Shuts down the server with 0 seconds of grace period. During the
        # grace period, the server won't accept new connections and allow
        # existing RPCs to continue within the grace period.
        await server.stop(0)


if __name__ == "__mai
```

steps.title: Confirm Graceful shutdown is not working for async server is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test grpc to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
`KeyboardInterrupt` exception will not be propagated into individual coroutines, instead it will be raised in the `asyncio.run` or `loop.run_until_complete` lines. This means my previous graceful shutdown example won't work, as reported in https://github.com/grpc/grpc/issues/26123.

There are 2 ways to clear-up for `KeyboardInterrupt` in asyncio:

1. Signal handlers;
2.
codeSnippets:
import logging
import asyncio
import grpc


async def serve() -> None:
    server = grpc.aio.server()
    listen_addr = "[::]:50051"
    server.add_insecure_port(listen_addr)
    logging.info("Starting server on %s", listen_addr)
    await server.start()
    try:
        await server.wait_for_termination()
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        # Shuts down the server with 0 seconds of grace period. During the
        # grace period, the server won't accept new connections and allow
        # existing RPCs to continue within the grace period.
        await server.stop(0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
    asyncio.run(serve())
